funding_carry.name: Funding-rate carry (delta-neutral)
funding_carry.economic_rationale: Spot long + perp short when funding is high: the perp shorts PAY me the funding. Real recurring cash flow, not a price prediction.

tests.note: pre-registered single config; pooled across 4 majors
pre-registered single config; pooled across 4 majors
Stage4 gauntlet 5/6 -> FAIL param_plateau (50% configs positive, param-sensitive). NOT deployable.
Continuous carry OVER-TRADES: net APR -4.14%/yr, 2/6. Selective(threshold) was better but fragile. Carry real but too small to robustly harvest at retail unleveraged w/ pessimistic costs.

basis_conv.name: Basis convergence
basis_conv.economic_rationale: Perp/futures price converges to spot at/over time; capture the spread as it closes.

liq_meanrev.name: Liquidation-cascade mean reversion
liq_meanrev.economic_rationale: Forced liquidations overshoot price; I provide liquidity into the cascade and fade it.

tests.note: REJECTED pre-backtest. Premise is directionally TRUE (78.4% of forced fills are worse than mark -> overshoot is REAL) but the discount is UNCAPTURABLE: pooled notional-weighted median +1.34bps vs 5bps bar. Fails at EVERY cost level incl. theoretical best case (2bps maker/maker -> net -0.66bps). ROOT CAUSE: discount is inversely proportional to size (Spearman -0.36). The <$10 bucket shows +74.5bps but is 0.20% of notional (avg fill $3.71); the $1k-10k bucket is 52.8% of notional and shows +0.70bps. Kraken EPP shreds liqs into 10% child orders absorbed near mark by resting MMs; only scraps that walk the book get a real discount, and scraps are not a business. NEW TRAP FOUND: the UNWEIGHTED median (+74.87bps on DOGE, +1.80 pooled) looks like a large edge. Notional-weighting collapses it to +2.60/+1.34. Any analysis of forced-flow fills without notional weighting will manufacture a fake edge.

liq_meanrev.notes: PRE-REGISTERED 2026-07-17, BEFORE seeing any data (Kraken PF_* liquidation tape). Decisive test is NOT a backtest: each execution carries markPrice at that instant, so dislocation = (fill-mark)/mark is directly observable. Sign folded by direction so adverse_bps>0 == filled WORSE than mark == a discount available to the other side. || FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION: if the premise ("forced liquidations overshoot") is true, median adverse_bps across liquidations must be POSITIVE and exceed round-trip maker cost (~4-5bps: Kraken maker ~2bps/side + spread). || PRE-SET BAR: median adverse_bps <= 0  => premise FALSE, reject outright, no backtest. 0 < median <= 5bps => real but uncapturable at retail cost, reject. median > 5bps => proceed to Stage 4 gauntlet. || NOTE the counter-evidence already in hand: the single record inspected pre-pull filled a forced Sell ~+0.6bps ABOVE mark (limitFilled=false), i.e. adverse_bps NEGATIVE. n=1, but it points against the premise. Recording this now so a later negative result cannot be reframed as expected.

vol_momo_alts.name: Vol-filtered momentum on mid-cap alts
vol_momo_alts.economic_rationale: Institutions avoid small caps for capacity reasons, leaving momentum less arbitraged; trade it only in calm-vol regimes to cut whipsaw.

tsmom.name: Time-series momentum (vol-scaled)
tsmom.economic_rationale: Trend persistence: past positive return predicts near-term positive (Liu-Tsyvinski up to 8wk). Prediction, not cash flow -- must survive brutal costs.

tests.note: Stage4 gauntlet 3/6 -> FAIL pbo(0.76)+dsr(0)+falsification(p=0.15). No real edge. Rejected.

tsmom.notes: Research: TSMOM>CSMOM in crypto but gross often insignificant net of costs.

xexch_arb.name: Cross-exchange funding arbitrage
xexch_arb.economic_rationale: Same perp, different exchanges, different funding (fragmented liquidity). Long the exchange paying longs + short the one paying shorts => market-neutral, collect the funding SPREAD. A structural edge, not a prediction.

tests.note: REJECTED. Mechanism REAL (fragmented liquidity does produce persistent funding differences) but UNCAPTURABLE: the spread mean-reverts in HOURS while the one-time round-trip cost needs DAYS to amortise. SURVIVAL: 42 episodes above 6% across 4 coins; 0 lasted past the 163h breakeven. Longest single episode = 13.8h (ETH) vs 163h needed — short by 12x. Medians: BTC 0.84h, ETH 7.24h, SOL 3.38h, XRP 0.84h. AUTOCORR of spread: 0.86 @10min, 0.25 @1h, NEGATIVE (-0.17) @8h => it does not decay, it FLIPS. Leg-pair flipped 9/7/15/28 times in 260 polls (each flip = a full round-trip cost). arb_calc net_at(30d) = +13.4% APR was fiction: it assumed a 30-day hold on a spread whose longest observed life is 13.8 HOURS. It also omitted the availability multiplier (SOL 70% => 6.7% became ~4.7%). Both now fixed. SAMPLE CAVEAT: 51h, ~6 independent OKX settlements. Small. But 42 episodes with 0 survivors and a 12x shortfall is not a marginal call. MEASUREMENT BUG ALSO FOUND: spread_pct subtracted Kraken (locked forward hourly rate) from OKX (still-updating 8h estimate) — two different objects. And Bybit was present in only 4/1036 rows (geo-blocked on GitHub runners), so the "cross-exchange" monitor was Kraken-vs-OKX only. KEY LESSON: we planned to wait 2-4 WEEKS for this answer. 2 DAYS sufficed — because the event we were waiting to observe happens 42 times in 2 days and never lasts. Short-lived phenomena need short windows, not long ones.

xexch_arb.notes: DISCIPLINE GAP: this ran as our ACTIVE BET from 2026-07-15 without ever being registered. Registered retroactively at the moment of its rejection. Every idea gets registered BEFORE it runs — this one did not, and that is a process failure worth recording, not hiding.

delist_event.name: Perp delisting forced-settlement event
delist_event.economic_rationale: Bybit force-closes ALL open positions in a delisted perp at a 30-min index average, on ~2 days notice. Holders cannot decline at any price, and their arb counterparties must unwind the hedged leg too. Market makers rationally withdraw from a contract with a known death date. So: price-insensitive forced exits into deliberately thinning liquidity. This is the purest FORCED-SETTLE in FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md (S1.3) and the first PERCENT-scale candidate we have tested — every prior rejection died because the edge was bps-scale and retail cost is ~5bps.

params.test: event study, BTC-adjusted, own-control (pre vs post drift)
params.sample: 193 single USDT-perp delistings; 186 usable, 6 relisted, 1 no-kline

tests.note: REJECTED — and the sign came out OPPOSITE to the prediction. Predicted: announcement -> negative abnormal return. Observed: the decline DECELERATES. pre-announcement abnormal drift median -4.31%, post -1.72%, so INCREMENTAL = +2.42% (volume-weighted +11.80%) vs a -0.50% bar. THE CONFOUND WAS THE WHOLE ANSWER: raw post-announcement return is -1.72%, which without the pre-drift control reads as a clean short signal. It is not. The coin was already falling faster BEFORE the news. Shorting on announcement yields +1.72% median; shorting the SAME coin 48h EARLIER yields +4.31%. The announcement makes you WORSE off by -2.59% — it is the worst moment to short, not the best. MECHANISM (monotonic, n=186, quartiles by pre-drift): worst-crash bucket pre -21.44% -> post -6.83% (incremental +17.30%); flat/up bucket pre +3.32% -> post +0.38% (incremental -3.15%). Textbook mean-reversion after a crash. REVERSE CAUSALITY: Bybit DDM auto-delists when last price < 20x tickSize, so the announcement is TRIGGERED BY the crash — a lagging indicator that arrives near the bottom. 45% of delisted coins were already down >5% vs BTC before the news. STRUCTURAL ERROR IN THE HYPOTHESIS ITSELF (the real lesson): I called delisting "price-insensitive forced selling". It is not selling at all. A perp is zero-net-supply — EVERY long is matched by a short. Delisting cash-settles BOTH sides at a 30-min index average. Cash settlement touches no order book, and the two sides net to zero by construction. There is no forced flow: there is forced SETTLEMENT, which is the OPPOSITE — a guarantee you need not trade at all. The only real flow is people electing to exit before settlement, which is two-sided and self-cancelling — the same category as the funding "dodge" (FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md 4.2). FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md 1.3 calls this "the purest forced flow that exists". That is WRONG and is now corrected. NOTE the weighting went the other way this time: volume-weighted incremental (+11.80%) is MORE positive than count-weighted (+2.42%) — in the larger coins the bounce is stronger still. Weighting is not always a haircut; it is just the truth.

delist_event.notes: PRE-REGISTERED 2026-07-17 BEFORE computing any return. Data recon only: 448 announcements in Bybit free API; klines for CLOSED symbols ARE retrievable with an explicit start/end (default returns latest bars = empty, which looks like no data but is not). || PREDICTION: announcement -> negative abnormal return, concentrated in the ~2 days between publishTime and the stated delisting time. || THE CONFOUND THAT DECIDES THIS (must be handled, not mentioned): SELECTION BIAS. Bybit delists coins that are ALREADY DYING - its own DDM rule delists when last price < 20x tickSize. So a post-announcement fall may be the coin simply continuing to die, and would have happened with no announcement at all. Therefore the measured quantity is NOT raw return. It is: (a) abnormal return vs BTC over the same window, AND (b) that abnormal return MINUS the same coins own pre-announcement abnormal drift. If the post-announcement drift merely continues the pre-announcement drift, the announcement carries NO information and the hypothesis is dead regardless of how negative the raw return looks. || PRE-SET BAR (fixed now, cannot be moved later): the incremental abnormal return (post-drift minus pre-drift) must be more negative than -50bps ROUND TRIP. 50bps is the honest cost for a DYING MICROCAP perp - 5.5bps taker each way is the fee, but the spread on a contract with a death date is the real cost, and it widens exactly when we would trade. If |incremental| <= 50bps => reject, uncapturable, same as the last three. || MUST ALSO SURVIVE (my own red flag from liq_meanrev, applied to myself): NOTIONAL WEIGHTING. If the effect exists only in coins with negligible traded volume, it is not a business - it is the .71-fill trap in a new costume. Report count-weighted AND volume-weighted; the volume-weighted number decides. || PRIOR: FORCED_FLOW_MAP.md S4.1 records that a predictable price response to PERP delisting is UNVERIFIED folklore - the literature covers spot/regulatory events, not perp forced settlement. The mechanism is certain; the price response is not. Expect rejection.
